title: Festive Creamy Jamaican Pineapple Curry
description: A vibrant and celebratory Jamaican curry featuring sweet roasted pineapple chunks simmered in a rich, spiced coconut milk broth. This fragrant vegan dish brings tropical warmth and festive flair to any dinner table.

ingredients.name: Scotch bonnet pepper
ingredients.notes: left whole for mild heat, or pierced for extra spice

instructions: Preheat your oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Toss the pineapple chunks with one tablespoon of melted coconut oil and roast them for 15 minutes until caramelized around the edges.
Heat the remaining coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat and saute the diced onion, garlic, and grated ginger for 5 minutes until soft.
Stir in the Jamaican curry powder and toast the spices for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the coconut milk and vegetable broth, stirring well to combine the toasted spices with the liquid.
Add the roasted pineapple, whole Scotch bonnet pepper, and fresh thyme sprigs to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer gently for 15 minutes until the curry thickens.
Discard the thyme sprigs and Scotch bonnet pepper before serving the warm curry over steamed rice.
